titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Joel Barlow Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11704410.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday April 24, 2018 at 20:18

did.unittitle: Joel Barlow Collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired from various sources. For more information see catalog cards filed in the collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Joel Barlow Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Joel Barlow Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call number: Za Barlow.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Barlow, Joel (1754-1812), American businessman, diplomat, and poet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection provides evidence of the personal and professional life of Joel Barlow between 1780 and 1813. Most notably the collection contains Barlow's continuing work on his epic poem The Vision of Columbus (1787), which he ultimately expanded and republished as The Columbiad (1807). The collection contains a printed version of The Vision of Columbus (Paris, 1793) with revisions and additional material, such as a list of subjects for illustration, estimates of printing costs, and preface. A letter to Barlow from Robert Fulton concerning copies The Columbiad found in a publisher's warehouse can be found in the correspondence series. The collection also includes autograph manuscript writings such as a chapter on "Means of Subsistence" from a larger work and a speech in response to the Republicans of Connecticut given at a political dinner.

In addition to documenting Barlow's writing career the papers also reflect his diplomatic service and business ventures. For example, in a letter to his wife Ruth Baldwin Barlow, Barlow discusses his experience as minister to Algiers. Several documents reflect Barlow's business interests, including a document composed while Barlow was serving as the European representative for the Scioto Associates. Barlow's increasing wealth can be seen in an indenture detailing the contents of his Washington, D.C. estate, Kalorama, created when Barlow leased his home to M. Serrurier, French minister to the U.S.

The collection contains several autograph letters written by Joel Barlow to his wife Ruth Baldwin Barlow, friends, and colleagues. Barlow corresponded with a number of notable people, including Sylvanus Bourne, Robert Fulton, Benjamin Latrobe, Fulwar Skipwith, and Noah Webster, among others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1780-1813. II. Writings, 1793-1811. III. Business Papers, 1790-1811.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains letters from Barlow to his wife Ruth Baldwin Barlow in addition to friends and colleagues. All letters are outgoing unless otherwise noted. The series also includes two third party letters, from Marie Joseph Paul Yves Roch Gilbert Du Motier, marquis de Lafayette, to Clara Baldwin, and from Presley Neville to Jonathan Russel (enclosed with Neville's letter to Barlow).

did.unittitle: Babcock, Elisha, autograph letter, signed, Washington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from Columbiad, Paris, 1813. Letter includes poem for use as Post-boy's New Year's address to readers of the American Mercury, Hartford.

did.unittitle: Barlow, Ruth Baldwin, autograph letter, signed, draft, Algiers, Algeria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Docketed in Barlow's hand: Copy / to Mrs. B.

did.unittitle: Barlow, Ruth Baldwin, 3 autograph letters, signed, to Joel Barlow, Hartford, Connecticut and Paris, France
Bishop, Abraham, autograph letter, signed, Kalorama, Washington
Bourne, Sylvanus, 3 letters, signed, Paris, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Each of the letters is in the hand of a different amanuensis and signed by Barlow.

did.unittitle: Bradley, Stephen R., autograph letter, signed, Paris, France
Clarke, Jabez, autograph letter, signed, Windham, Connecticut
Cutts, Richard, autograph letter, signed, Paris, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns watch that Congressman Cutts commissioned Barlow to buy.

did.unittitle: Dearborn, Honorable Henry, autograph letter, signed, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Fulton, Robert, autograph letter, signed, Kalorama, Washington
Fulton, Robert, autograph note to Joel Barlow, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, concerning copies of Barlow's Columbiad found in the publisher's warehouse.
Hopkins, Samuel M., autograph letter, signed, Paris, France
Latrobe, Benjamin, Paris, France
Lee, William, autograph letter, signed, London, England
Meigs, Josiah, autograph letter, signed, Kalorama, Washington
Lafayette, Marie Joseph Paul Yves Roch Gilbert Du Motier, marquis de, to Clara Baldwin, autograph letter, signed
Lafayette, Marie Joseph Paul Yves Roch Gilbert Du Motier, marquis de, to Ruth Baldwin Barlow
Maret, Hugues-Bernard, duc de Bassano, 65 letters to Joel Barlow, Paris, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In French; written as Napoleon's Minister of foreign affairs to Barlow as American ambassador to Paris. The letters are docketed with notes of their content, and three are accompanied with contemporary manuscript translation into English.

did.unittitle: Madison, Dolly, autograph letter, signed, from Ruth Baldwin Barlow, New York
National Institute of France, autograph letter, signed, copy, Kalorama, Washington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presenting a copy of his Columbiad, with, on verso, Barlow's copy of the reply of the Institute (Joachim Le Breton, perpetual secretary), Paris, France, October 15, 1808.

did.unittitle: Neville, Presley, autograph letter, signed, to Barlow, Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, docketed in Barlow's hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes autograph letter, signed, from Presley Neville to Jonathan Russell, care of Barlow, dated June 7, 1811.

did.unittitle: Phillips, Richard, autograph letter, signed, Kalorama, Washington
Skipwith, Fulwar, letter from Ruth Baldwin Barlow, Paris, France
Skipwith, Fulwar, 4 autograph letters, signed, Paris, France
Watson, James, 3 autograph letters, signed, London, England, and Paris, France
Webster, Noah, 3 autograph letters, signed, Hartford, Connecticut
Writings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains autograph manuscripts by Barlow as well as heavily annotated printed versions of his The Vision of Columbus .

did.unittitle: Early version, printed version (Paris: Printed at The English Press, 1793), corrected. Consists of octavo printed pages with handwritten corrections, interleaved with quarto sheets bearing additional revisions, with occasional octavo and smaller leaves tipped or pasted in. Preliminary material includes list of subjects for illustration, estimates of cost of printing, and preface.

Intermediate version, printed version (Paris: Printed at The English Press, 1793), corrected, interleaved with quarto sheets bearing additional revisions. Preliminary material includes dedication, list of subjects for illustration, and preface. An index of names and a list of corrections appear at the end.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of The New England Quarterly with an article by Ezra K. Maxfield on the manuscript.

did.unittitle: Final version, printed version (Paris: Printed at The English Press, 1793), corrected. Incorporates occasional octavo printed pages with smaller leaves tipped or pasted in. The notes and an index of names appear at the end.
Lines Presented With a Rose To Miss Anica Preble, autograph manuscript
Means of Subsistence, Chapter VI, autograph manuscript, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of Barlow's definition of "a state of nature," the beginning of a chapter of a proposed work on political science.

did.unittitle: Notes, autograph manuscript, chiefly on Barlow's reading
Response to an address from the Republicans of Connecticut, autograph manuscript, corrected, given at a dinner arranged by Abraham Bishop in Barlow's honor
Business Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of a number of business documents, including a document relating to the Scioto Associates, a consular certificate detailing goods being sent from France to the U.S., a financial document, and an indenture relating to Barlow's property Kalorama.

did.unittitle: Autograph document, signed, a translation into English of a document in French executed by Barlow as agent and attorney of Manassah Culter, Winthrop Sargent, and William Duer, and F. M. J. de Barth and A. Coquet, purchasers of the lands of Scioto. With Barlow's certification of comparison with the original, Paris, France
Consular certificate, executed by Stephen Cathalan, Jr., American vice-consul, with Barlow's handwritten declaration concerning his shipment of various goods from France to the United States

Printed form of the Caisse d'escompte du commerce, Paris, for the payment of 3,000 francs, filled in and signed by Barlow. Tipped to the document is an unsigned manuscript note, relating to the payment of the order.
Indenture between Barlow and M. Serrurier, French minister to the U.S., leasing Kalorama for a period of three years, with inventory of furniture in the house.
Oversize
Lafayette, Marie Joseph Paul Yves Roch Gilbert Du Motier, marquis de, to Ruth Baldwin Barlow
Madison, Dolly, autograph letter, signed, New York
